author: OC Nodes
msg: Technically at 20c daily per license if you get them all working then you are already making $1200/month. The bottle neck is getting the word out now without a referral program. We will get there. What we are missing is more communication to keep things interesting and allow us to plan better. I would have liked to establish a brand already and start aggressive marketing but this move is now postponed due to lack of communication about clear targets and timelines. They are doing good but just need to work on this better. New tasks and higher rewards are inevitable.. we are already at semi -sustainable semi profitable territory which is amazing given how new this project is

author: HFA | Earnest Nodes
msg: I agree with this but how to convince people about this when no actual evidence of reasonable earnings available

author: Coffee Nodes
msg: look at the adoption curve of any new tech. We're at the start, people don't know, don't really believe and the few people here are early adopters. If/when rewards increase, finding people won't be difficult,  until then, we can only wait and hope tbh. Once people earn 30-50+ dollars a month per license, we will have too many people wanting licenses and not enough licenses

author: OC Nodes
msg: Unfortunately now we can't without a referral program. Otherwise with a few hundred dollars you could run a crazy effective ad campaign on Instagram in third world countries and probably get tons of people taking your licenses.. but now you can't tag your licenses. Those $4-6 monthly, once they scale a tad bit more will be VERY attractive in Africa, India, etc.. just need a referral system

author: OC Nodes
msg: It will be way before 30-50.. esp in poor countries

author: Byron
msg:
who says "there is no rush"? Since us investors and UNOs are expected to do the heavy lifting of marketing/promoting our licenses, we need every tool available YESTERDAY. Tools that the team should have provided from the get go (in the first place - but I do not want to go there again!).

So having the team decouple from implementing these - super mandatory - tools, and give them time to focus on new tasks and revenue, will surely move this project way forward to my opinion.

Regarding GDPR, I do not see how GDPR would be an issue for using the license management API. Of course the service would be GDPR compliant meaning that UNOs and affiliates will need to sign up using their email and/or web3 wallet to use the service and if they decide to stop using it all their data will be deleted.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
I think a problem here would be the implementation. Depending on what incentives the license operators will have for referring someone these are either external or baked into the rewards they get through Unity. If its the latter, then this is something that you probably cant implement with API access 🤔

Do you have concrete ideas how you would set up the referral system? I know the team is still finalizing it and input never hurts 😃 I also find this to be a very important element to get ULOs onboarded and have some ideas how I would implement it. But there are various different routes you could go with this and each has their advantages and disadvantages.

author: Byron
msg:
Exactly, affiliates could be ULOs or not. So you are correct, rewards could target leased license(s) of the UNO(s) they are affiliated with (mainly in the form of increased rev share) or they could be external, where the UNO sends a specific reward to the affiliate directly (if the affiliate is not a ULO, or they have selected the specific reward type). In all cases (and especially if Unity license management API does not provide the option for the UNO to adjust the share of a leased license mid flight) all rewards could be distributed as external (wallet to wallet).

Yes I have concrete and super cool ideas, but I do not generally do things free of charge and I highly doubt that the team will pay for this kind of feedback. keep in mind that this is not going to be a free service (a small commission to UNOs would be applied for every new referral they acquire using the service)

author: James_WM_ENO
msg: I thought it might work like the rewards may be split 3 ways.. say you set a licence at 50/50 then when one of your ULO’s find someone they can generate their own code that will send the next person to one of your licences, then perhaps a small % like say 2% would go to the original ULO, and 48% to the new ULO

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg:
Thats my preferred style as well. 

 ULO referrs another ULO and gets a share of their rewards. This increases their rewards on which they then pay the predetermined share to their UNO. 

If a refferd ULO reffers another ULO, then the referrals rewards slowly creep up the chain. 

1. This would incentivize each ULO to get refferals, because you earn most on your direct referrals.

2. This would also incentive a ULO to make sure their referrals are actually active, because their reward is based on the revenue their referrals get.

author: Quoia75
msg: Submitted this feature request a while back - a warning system that lets the ULO know they are falling below the required uptime min. At least 24 hours before auto-revoke. That time frame should be customizable to fit each UNO's requirements. I think a perma-ban solution should also be available for ULOs who abuse the system by instantly trying to re-lease from the same UNO when they have proven not to be a quality operator.

author: OC Nodes
msg: I agree with letting UNO set whatever split they want. I'm also with the idea that a lease is a lease and shouldn't be broken unless pre specified conditions are met. My goal is to grow the network and once it's time to renew the lease the UNO can then increase his portion as rewards scale up. No "price caps" and "rent controls".. its not necessary. The market will adjust. As rewards go up there will be no need for 10:90 and then 50:50 becomes very reasonable

author: OC Nodes
msg: Yeah. This is a long long term game.. I'm totally fine with leaving some on the table if it means growing the network as rapidly as possible. Currently rewards don't support 50:50 leases period. They can get claimed but I'm sure they will get abandoned sooner or later. 10-20:90-80 is viable for ULOs. After a few months when rewards are higher then new leases will issue. You don't have to set 12 months but can do 4-6 to factor for anticipated higher rewards

author: Miro K
msg:
Are you part of those UNOs who right away would expect automatic 96usd per phone? 
We only got live for android during November. I can not realistically see a fully developed app beforehand as real unos and ulos makes all the differenceinproduction app and only data from production app can fully leverage complex b2b deals. Not a short sales cycle here.I saw it for what it was - tech demo, PoC. I got no patience for get rich fast guys. By any investments standards once we get to 0.5 USD perdat per licence, this is beyond life Roi. 
Also I don’t expect weekly but rather monthly updates. To give weekly updates it might be actually really counterproductive.

author: Marco
msg: It would be nice if there were a tool like a speed test built in to the app that send/received dummy packets similar to the telemetry tasks and could give us a rating on connection specific to tasks. Testing speed is easy, but making tweaks to network settings then waiting 24-36 hours to see if it made an impact on rewards seems inefficient.

msg: It would make validating hardware or service changes very easy and help troubleshoot with ULOs
